readiness_rationale: The structural pattern is proven: led-0002 (verified) confirms tools/ledger-check.py is a working template (argparse + jsonschema Draft202012Validator + per-line/structural checks). The plan mirrors it for policies and adds the readiness-gate cross-check from led-0003. Called policy_candidate, not ready: the readiness-gate behavior (AC-4/AC-5) rests partly on led-0003 which is still 'assumed' until its oracle goes green.

grounded: led-0002 (verified): tools/ledger-check.py is the working template to mirror.
led-0001 (verified): contract-policy-check v1 active with 5 oracle-backed ACs.
tentative: led-0003 (assumed): 'ready only if every rests_on maps to a verified entry' - a design rule, oracle AC-4 not green yet.

candidate_steps.action: Write schemas/policy.schema.json: require status(enum)/readiness_rationale/evidence_audit/reachability_audit/candidate_steps/recommended_next_action; candidate_steps items require action/rests_on/stop_or_remeasure_if; additionalProperties allowed.
candidate_steps.expected_effect: AC-2 (illegal status) and AC-3 (missing readiness_rationale) flip red->green once the validator loads this schema.
stop_or_remeasure_if: a good fixture is wrongly rejected (schema too strict)

candidate_steps.action: Write tools/policy-check.py mirroring ledger-check.py: load+validate schema, then if status=='ready' enforce the readiness gate (every rests_on id from candidate_steps + recommended_next_action must exist in --ledger with authority=='verified').
candidate_steps.expected_effect: AC-1 and AC-5 flip green (well-formed + ready-all-verified pass); AC-4 flips green (ready with an assumed rests_on rejected).
stop_or_remeasure_if: AC-4 passes when it should reject (gate not enforced)

recommended_next_action.action: Write schemas/policy.schema.json first (smallest change that flips AC-2 and AC-3).
recommended_next_action.why: Mirrors the proven ledger-check pattern (led-0002); lowest-risk first oracle flips.
stop_or_remeasure_if: a good fixture is wrongly rejected
